title: Plans, Limits & Entitlements Editable Without Deploy

tasks.title: Add typed plan-attribute columns to qzpay-drizzle schema
tasks.description:
In the sibling qzpay repo (`/proyects/PACKAGES/qzpay/packages/drizzle/src/schema/plans.schema.ts`), add three typed columns to the `billingPlans` table: `displayName varchar(255) not null`, `monthlyPriceArs integer not null`, `annualPriceArs integer nullable`. These currently live only in the `metadata` jsonb column. Bump the `@qazuor/qzpay-drizzle` package version and publish. Verify via `drizzle-kit generate` in the qzpay repo that the new columns produce a clean migration diff. This is a cross-repo change — coordinate the publish before Hospeda-side work (T-002) can consume it.

Test requirements: qzpay repo's own schema unit test (if any) covering the new columns' types/nullability; no Hospeda-side test needed for this task.

subtasks.title: Add displayName/monthlyPriceArs/annualPriceArs columns to plans.schema.ts
Run drizzle-kit generate in qzpay repo to confirm clean diff
Bump qzpay-drizzle package version
Publish new qzpay-drizzle version

tasks.title: Bump qzpay-drizzle pin and install in Hospeda
tasks.description:
Update the `@qazuor/qzpay-drizzle` version pin in `packages/db/package.json` to the version published in T-001, then run `pnpm install` at the repo root to update the lockfile. Verify `packages/db/src/billing/drizzle-adapter.ts` still type-checks against the new schema shape (it re-exports `createQZPayDrizzleAdapter`, no code change expected, just a type surface check).

Test requirements: none new — `pnpm typecheck --filter=@repo/db` must pass as verification.

subtasks.title: Bump version in packages/db/package.json
Run pnpm install
Verify drizzle-adapter.ts typechecks

tasks.title: Write migration promoting metadata fields to typed columns
tasks.description:
Run `pnpm db:generate` to produce a Drizzle migration in `packages/db/src/migrations/` adding `display_name`, `monthly_price_ars`, `annual_price_ars` columns to `billing_plans` (per the new qzpay-drizzle schema from T-002). Hand-extend the generated SQL with a backfill `UPDATE billing_plans SET display_name = metadata->>'displayName', monthly_price_ars = (metadata->>'monthlyPriceArs')::integer, annual_price_ars = (metadata->>'annualPriceArs')::integer WHERE ...` for existing rows (additive migration, no DROP of metadata). Apply via `pnpm db:migrate` locally and verify all 9 existing plans have correct backfilled values.

Test requirements: integration test in `packages/db/test/` verifying, after migration, the 3 new columns are populated correctly for every existing seeded plan (compare against known metadata values).

subtasks.title: Run pnpm db:generate for the new columns
Add backfill UPDATE statement to the generated migration
Apply locally via pnpm db:migrate and verify backfill
Write migration backfill integration test

tasks.title: Repoint MODEL_C_FIELD_SPLIT at new typed columns
tasks.description:
In `packages/billing/src/config/model-c-field-split.ts`, replace the `'metadata.displayName'`, `'metadata.monthlyPriceArs'`, `'metadata.annualPriceArs'` keys with `'displayName'`, `'monthlyPriceArs'`, `'annualPriceArs'` (the new column names from T-003), keeping their `'commercial'` classification unchanged. Update the JSDoc comments to reference the column, not the jsonb path. This keeps the exhaustiveness guard (AC-2.3) accurate for the new schema shape.

Test requirements: update `packages/billing/test/model-c-field-split.test.ts` (the existing guard test) to expect the new key names; run it to confirm it still passes against the seed's field detection.

subtasks.title: Rename the 3 metadata.* keys to their new column names
Update JSDoc comments
Update and run the guard test

tasks.title: Update seed sync to read/write typed plan-attribute columns
tasks.description:
In `packages/seed/src/required/billingPlans.seed.ts`, update `detectDivergences` and `ensurePlan` to read/write `displayName`, `monthlyPriceArs`, `annualPriceArs` as top-level columns instead of `metadata.*` paths, consistent with T-004's reclassified keys. The Model C sync behavior (commercial = DB wins, seed never overwrites) must be preserved for these 3 fields — only the storage location changes, not the sync policy.

Test requirements: update existing seed unit tests covering Model C sync for these 3 fields (config-vs-DB divergence scenarios) to assert against the new columns instead of metadata jsonb paths.

subtasks.title: Update detectDivergences for the 3 fields
Update ensurePlan write path for the 3 fields
Update seed sync unit tests

tasks.title: Add DB-backed plan-attribute read methods to PlanService
tasks.description:
CANCELLED (spec-realign 2026-07-02): already implemented via SPEC-168/SPEC-192/SPEC-211, shipped before this task list was written. In `packages/service-core/src/services/billing/` (verify exact filename, likely `plan.service.ts` — create if it doesn't exist yet as a dedicated file), add/extend read methods that select `description`, `active`, `displayName`, `monthlyPriceArs`, `annualPriceArs` directly from `billing_plans` DB rows (via the qzpay adapter), returning the shape currently produced by `ALL_PLANS` entries for these fields. This is the shared read layer T-007/T-010/T-011 build on.

Test requirements: unit tests in the service's test file covering: returns correct shape for an existing plan, returns not-found for a missing plan id, correctly maps the 3 typed columns (not metadata) into the response.

subtasks.title: Locate or create plan.service.ts in service-core billing services
Implement DB-backed read method for plan attributes

tasks.title: Implement admin edit-plan-attributes mutation with atomic price write
tasks.description:
CANCELLED (spec-realign 2026-07-02): already implemented via SPEC-168/SPEC-192/SPEC-211, shipped before this task list was written. In `PlanService`, add a mutation accepting `{ description?, active?, displayName?, monthlyPriceArs?, annualPriceArs? }`, validated by a new Zod schema in `@repo/schemas` (e.g. `billing-plan-attributes-update.schema.ts`). Per Q4=A, write `billing_plans` and the matching `billing_prices` row atomically in a single DB transaction — both succeed or neither does. Reject negative prices and empty `displayName`.

Test requirements: unit tests covering successful atomic update (both tables reflect the change), transaction rollback on partial failure (simulate a `billing_prices` write error, assert `billing_plans` is unchanged), Zod rejection of negative price and empty displayName.

subtasks.title: Create Zod schema for attribute updates
Implement atomic transaction (billing_plans + billing_prices)
Write unit tests incl. rollback scenario

tasks.title: Implement admin edit-plan-limit-values mutation
tasks.description:
CANCELLED (spec-realign 2026-07-02): already implemented via SPEC-168/SPEC-192/SPEC-211, shipped before this task list was written. In `PlanService`, add a mutation accepting a partial `Record<LimitKey, number>`. Validate every key against the `LimitKey` enum (reject unknown keys) and every value is a non-negative integer. Write to `billing_plans.limits` jsonb, merging with existing keys (do not clobber limit keys not included in the update — per Model C, `limitsKeysPresent` stays capability/config-driven, only `limitsValues` is commercial/admin-edited here). Independent of the T-001..T-006 migration chain since `limits` is already a top-level DB column.

Test requirements: unit tests for partial update preserving untouched keys, rejection of a key not in `LimitKey`, rejection of a negative value.

subtasks.title: Create Zod schema for limit-value updates
Implement merge-preserving write to limits jsonb
Write unit tests

tasks.title: Switch addon-entitlement limit-value lookup to DB
tasks.description:
CANCELLED (spec-realign 2026-07-02): already implemented via SPEC-168/SPEC-192/SPEC-211, shipped before this task list was written. In `apps/api/src/services/addon-entitlement.service.ts` (around line 160, per the spec's Q2 implication), change the limit-VALUE lookup from `ALL_PLANS.find(p => p.slug === slug).limits` to a DB read (via qzpay `billing.plans.get()` or the new PlanService read method). Limit KEYS stay type-safe from the `LimitKey` enum — only the numeric VALUE resolution moves to DB, per Q2=A. Independent of the metadata-column migration (T-001..T-006) since `limits` is already a DB column.

Test requirements: update the existing unit tests for this service to mock a DB read instead of the config lookup; add a test verifying a limit value changed in the DB (not config) is what gets returned.

subtasks.title: Replace ALL_PLANS.find lookup with DB read
Update/add unit tests mocking the DB read

Resolves the deferred bug #8 from the SPEC-143 smoke session (PR #1215).

Test requirements: update the route's existing test to assert the response now reflects DB row values (not `ALL_PLANS`), including a case where a DB row's displayName/price differs from the config default.

tasks.title: Wire Cloudflare revalidation trigger on plan save
tasks.description:

On successful save from T-015 or T-016's mutations (server-side, in `PlanService` after the DB write commits), call the existing revalidation service (`initializeRevalidationService()` pattern, `apps/api/src/index.ts:84`) to purge the Cloudflare cache for `/suscriptores/planes/` and the tourist pricing path, per Q5=C. No API plan-cache to invalidate (Q7 = none exists).

Test requirements: unit test verifying the revalidation service is called with the correct paths after a successful attribute or limit-value save.

tasks.title: Integration test: admin attribute edit reflects live without deploy
tasks.description:
SCOPE REVISED (spec-realign 2026-07-02): the functionality this test/audit targets already exists (shipped via SPEC-168/192/211) — verify EXISTING behavior, do not assume it needs to be built. End-to-end integration test: call the admin edit-attributes mutation (T-007) via its API route, then immediately call the public listPlans endpoint (T-011) and assert the new value is present — no cache, no deploy, no restart involved.

Test requirements: integration test in `apps/api/test/` (or the project's e2e-style integration suite) covering the full write→read round trip for `displayName` and `monthlyPriceArs`.

tasks.title: Document the narrowed Model C admin-editable field policy
tasks.description:
Update packages/billing/CLAUDE.md and docs/guides/managing-billing-plans.md to document: the 3 newly-typed commercial columns, the Q0 conflict resolution (sortOrder/trial/entitlements moved from capability to commercial per the T-024..T-027 live-bug fix, category/isDefault removed from the editable surface entirely), the new MODEL_C_FIELD_SPLIT runtime guard on PlanService.update(), and the SSR+revalidation pattern already in place for pricing pages. Cross-reference .specs/HOS-39-plans-limits-entitlements-editable/spec.md.

Test requirements: none (docs-only task); verify markdown lint passes if the project enforces it.

subtasks.title: Update packages/billing/CLAUDE.md
Update docs/guides/managing-billing-plans.md

tasks.title: Audit and prune now-dead ALL_PLANS display-surface references
tasks.description:
SCOPE REVISED (spec-realign 2026-07-02): the functionality this test/audit targets already exists (shipped via SPEC-168/192/211) — verify EXISTING behavior, do not assume it needs to be built. Grep the 5 originally-identified display-surface files (spec section 2.1) plus any new call sites introduced during this spec, and confirm none still read `ALL_PLANS` for display purposes. Leave the section 2.2 internal-logic references untouched (they intentionally stay on config per Q6=B). Remove any now-unused imports.

Test requirements: none new (a pure grep/prune audit); existing test suite must still pass after removal.

subtasks.title: Grep for remaining ALL_PLANS display-surface usages
Remove unused imports/dead code
Run full existing test suite to confirm no regression

tasks.title: Reclassify entitlements/sortOrder/hasTrial/trialDays to commercial in Model C
tasks.description:
In `packages/billing/src/config/model-c-field-split.ts`, change `entitlements`, `metadata.sortOrder`, `metadata.hasTrial`, `metadata.trialDays` from `'capability'` to `'commercial'` in `MODEL_C_FIELD_SPLIT`. Update the JSDoc on each entry to explain why: these fields are already editable via `PlanService.update()`/`PlanDialog.tsx` (shipped by SPEC-168), so the seed must stop overwriting them - DB wins, matching what the admin UI already implies. `category` and `isDefault` stay `capability` (see T-026 - they get removed from the editable surface instead of reclassified).

Test requirements: run `packages/billing/test/model-c-field-split.test.ts` (the exhaustiveness guard) to confirm it still passes against the updated classification.

subtasks.title: Reclassify the 4 fields to commercial
Update JSDoc explaining the rationale
Run guard test

tasks.title: Stop seed sync from overwriting the 4 reclassified fields
tasks.description:
In `packages/seed/src/required/billingPlans.seed.ts`'s `buildCapabilitySyncPayload()` (around lines 245-322): remove the `entitlements` capability-sync block (currently lines 253-255), and remove `'metadata.sortOrder'`, `'metadata.hasTrial'`, `'metadata.trialDays'` from the hardcoded `META_CAPABILITY_FIELDS` array (keep only `'metadata.category'` and `'metadata.isDefault'`, which stay capability). This closes the actual bug - the classification table alone (T-024) does not change sync behavior since this function has its own hardcoded field list.

Test requirements: seed sync unit tests verifying that when a DB row's `entitlements`/`sortOrder`/`hasTrial`/`trialDays` diverge from config (simulating an operator edit), a seed re-run does NOT revert them - the previous behavior (revert) must now fail these tests without the fix.

subtasks.title: Remove entitlements capability-sync block
Remove 3 fields from META_CAPABILITY_FIELDS
Write/update seed sync tests proving edits are preserved

tasks.title: Remove category/isDefault from UpdatePlanInput
tasks.description:
In `packages/service-core/src/services/billing/plan/plan.types.ts`, remove `category` and `isDefault` from `UpdatePlanInput` (lines ~62, ~74) - these are `capability`-layer per Model C and were never actually requested as admin-editable; their presence in the update type is an oversight, not a deliberate feature. Verify `updatePlan()` in `plan.crud.ts` doesn't specifically process these two fields (if it does, remove that handling too). Check `apps/admin/src/features/billing-plans/components/PlanDialog.tsx` for whether its edit form submits `category`/`isDefault` - if so, remove those inputs from the edit (not create) mode.

Test requirements: TypeScript compilation is the primary guard (removing the fields makes passing them a compile error). Add/update a unit test asserting `updatePlan()`'s accepted input shape no longer includes these keys.

subtasks.title: Remove category/isDefault from UpdatePlanInput
Remove any dead handling in updatePlan()
Check and fix PlanDialog.tsx edit-mode form if needed

tasks.title: Add runtime Model C guard to PlanService.update()
tasks.description:
In `plan.crud.ts`'s `updatePlan()` (or its entry point in `plan.service.ts`), add a runtime check that validates the incoming update payload's keys against `MODEL_C_FIELD_SPLIT`: any key classified `'capability'` must be rejected with `ServiceError(VALIDATION_ERROR, ...)`. This is the structural fix - it makes `UpdatePlanInput` and `MODEL_C_FIELD_SPLIT` impossible to silently drift apart again for any FUTURE field someone adds, closing the root cause of this bug class, not just today's instance.

Test requirements: unit test that calls `updatePlan()` with a capability-layer key (bypass TS via an unsafe cast, simulating a future field that hasn't been removed from the type yet) and asserts it throws `VALIDATION_ERROR`. Unit test confirming all current commercial-layer fields (description, active, displayName, monthlyPriceArs, annualPriceArs, limits) still pass through successfully.

subtasks.title: Add MODEL_C_FIELD_SPLIT validation check to updatePlan()
Write unit test for capability-field rejection
Write unit test confirming commercial fields still pass
